title: The Group Chat
text_zh: 办公室有一个微信群。三十二个人。每天早上，老周在群里发一条消息：“早上好！今天也要加油！”每天都有人回复“加油”。但不是每天都是同一个人。小梅统计了一下，回复的人平均每天只有三个。而且从来没有人回复两次。她跟方阿姨说了这件事。方阿姨说：“老周知道吗？”小梅说：“他觉得大家都很积极。”方阿姨想了一下，说：“也对。三个人回复和三十二个人回复，对老周来说是一样的。”
text_pinyin: Bàngōngshì yǒu yí gè wēixìn qún. Sānshí'èr gè rén. Měi tiān zǎoshang, Lǎo Zhōu zài qún lǐ fā yì tiáo xiāoxi: 'Zǎoshang hǎo! Jīntiān yě yào jiāyóu!' Měi tiān dōu yǒu rén huífù 'jiāyóu'. Dàn bú shì měi tiān dōu shì tóng yí gè rén. Xiǎo Méi tǒngjì le yíxià, huífù de rén píngjūn měi tiān zhǐ yǒu sān gè. Érqiě cónglái méi yǒu rén huífù liǎng cì. Tā gēn Fāng āyí shuō le zhè jiàn shì. Fāng āyí shuō: 'Lǎo Zhōu zhīdào ma?' Xiǎo Méi shuō: 'Tā juéde dàjiā dōu hěn jījí.' Fāng āyí xiǎng le yíxià, shuō: 'Yě duì. Sān gè rén huífù hé sānshí'èr gè rén huífù, duì Lǎo Zhōu lái shuō shì yíyàng de.'
text_en: The office has a WeChat group. Thirty-two people. Every morning, Old Zhou sends a message: 'Good morning! Let's do our best today too!' Every day someone replies 'let's go.' But it's never the same person. Xiao Mei ran the numbers — on average only three people reply per day. And nobody has ever replied twice. She mentioned this to Auntie Fang. Fang said: 'Does Zhou know?' Mei said: 'He thinks everyone is very enthusiastic.' Fang thought for a moment and said: 'Fair enough. Three replies or thirty-two — for Zhou, it's the same.'

questions.q_zh: 方阿姨最后那句话是什么意思？
questions.q_en: What does Fang's last line mean?

options.text: 老周的乐观跟实际数据没有关系
options.pinyin: Lǎo Zhōu de lèguān gēn shíjì shùjù méi yǒu guānxi
options.text_en: Zhou's optimism has no relationship to actual data

options.pinyin: sānshí'èr gè rén dōu huífù le

options.text: 方阿姨觉得老周说得对
options.pinyin: Fāng āyí juéde Lǎo Zhōu shuō de duì
wēixìn qún rén tài duō le
options.text_en: the WeChat group has too many people

questions.explanation: Fang's observation is sharp: Zhou's confidence doesn't scale with evidence. Whether 3 or 32 people reply, his enthusiasm stays the same — which means the data was never relevant to his feeling in the first place.

title: The Thermos
text_zh: 罗老师给每个人带了一个保温杯，上面印着“健康生活，从喝热水开始”。她花了两个星期选这个杯子。小梅收到了，说：“谢谢。我不喝热水。”罗老师愣了一下。“你不喝热水？”小梅说：“嗯。我喝冰水。”罗老师觉得这是态度问题。小梅觉得这是温度问题。罗老师说：“我花了两个星期挑的。”小梅说：“杯子很好看。但是我不喝热水。”两个人都没有错。一个人在说心意，一个人在说事实。
text_pinyin: Luó lǎoshī gěi měi gè rén dài le yí gè bǎowēn bēi, shàngmiàn yìn zhe 'Jiànkāng shēnghuó, cóng hē rè shuǐ kāishǐ'. Tā huā le liǎng gè xīngqī xuǎn zhè ge bēizi. Xiǎo Méi shōu dào le, shuō: 'Xièxie. Wǒ bù hē rè shuǐ.' Luó lǎoshī lèng le yíxià. 'Nǐ bù hē rè shuǐ?' Xiǎo Méi shuō: 'Èn. Wǒ hē bīng shuǐ.' Luó lǎoshī juéde zhè shì tàidù wèntí. Xiǎo Méi juéde zhè shì wēndù wèntí. Luó lǎoshī shuō: 'Wǒ huā le liǎng gè xīngqī tiāo de.' Xiǎo Méi shuō: 'Bēizi hěn hǎokàn. Dànshì wǒ bù hē rè shuǐ.' Liǎng gè rén dōu méi yǒu cuò. Yí gè rén zài shuō xīnyì, yí gè rén zài shuō shìshí.
text_en: Teacher Luo brought everyone a thermos, printed with 'Healthy living starts with hot water.' She spent two weeks choosing it. Xiao Mei received hers and said: 'Thanks. I don't drink hot water.' Luo paused. 'You don't drink hot water?' Mei said: 'No. I drink ice water.' Luo felt this was an attitude problem. Mei felt this was a temperature problem. Luo said: 'I spent two weeks picking these out.' Mei said: 'The thermos is nice-looking. But I don't drink hot water.' Neither was wrong. One was talking about intention, the other about fact.

questions.q_zh: 为什么两个人说不到一起？
questions.q_en: Why can't the two people understand each other?

options.text: 一个人在说感情，一个人在说实际情况
options.pinyin: yí gè rén zài shuō gǎnqíng, yí gè rén zài shuō shíjì qíngkuàng
options.text_en: one is talking about feelings, the other about practical reality

options.pinyin: Xiǎo Méi bù xǐhuān Luó lǎoshī
bǎowēn bēi zhìliàng bù hǎo
Luó lǎoshī bù yīnggāi sòng lǐwù

questions.explanation: Luo hears rejection of her effort; Mei is stating a preference. Luo's frame is emotional (I cared enough to choose this), Mei's is literal (I don't use hot water). Both are valid — the friction is that they're having two different conversations.

title: The Old Recipe
text_zh: 老白说红烧肉要用冰糖，因为他妈妈一直用冰糖。老周说白糖也可以，网上很多视频都用白糖。老白说：“网上的东西能信吗？我妈妈做了四十年。”老周说：“时代不一样了。现在有更好的办法。”老白说：“什么叫更好？好吃才是好。我妈妈做的最好吃。”老周说：“你妈妈用冰糖是因为以前买不到白糖。”老白不说话了。不是因为他觉得老周说得对，而是因为他不想把妈妈拿来讨论。
text_pinyin: Lǎo Bái shuō hóngshāo ròu yào yòng bīngtáng, yīnwèi tā māma yìzhí yòng bīngtáng. Lǎo Zhōu shuō báitáng yě kěyǐ, wǎng shàng hěn duō shìpín dōu yòng báitáng. Lǎo Bái shuō: 'Wǎng shàng de dōngxi néng xìn ma? Wǒ māma zuò le sìshí nián.' Lǎo Zhōu shuō: 'Shídài bù yíyàng le. Xiànzài yǒu gèng hǎo de bànfǎ.' Lǎo Bái shuō: 'Shénme jiào gèng hǎo? Hǎo chī cái shì hǎo. Wǒ māma zuò de zuì hǎo chī.' Lǎo Zhōu shuō: 'Nǐ māma yòng bīngtáng shì yīnwèi yǐqián mǎi bú dào báitáng.' Lǎo Bái bù shuōhuà le. Bú shì yīnwèi tā juéde Lǎo Zhōu shuō de duì, ér shì yīnwèi tā bù xiǎng bǎ māma ná lái tǎolùn.
text_en: Uncle Bai said braised pork must use rock sugar, because his mother always used rock sugar. Old Zhou said white sugar works too — lots of online videos use white sugar. Bai said: 'Can you trust what's online? My mother cooked for forty years.' Zhou said: 'Times have changed. Now there are better methods.' Bai said: 'What does better mean? Tasting good is what matters. My mother's was the best.' Zhou said: 'Your mother used rock sugar because white sugar wasn't available back then.' Bai went quiet. Not because he thought Zhou was right, but because he didn't want his mother turned into a debate point.

questions.q_zh: 老白最后不说话的原因是什么？
questions.q_en: Why did Bai stop talking at the end?

options.text: 他不想让妈妈的记忆变成争论的材料
options.pinyin: tā bù xiǎng ràng māma de jìyì biàn chéng zhēnglùn de cáiliào
options.text_en: he didn't want his mother's memory to become debate material

options.pinyin: tā juéde Lǎo Zhōu shuō de duì
tā bú huì zuò hóngshāo ròu
tā shēngqì le

questions.explanation: The argument was never really about sugar. For Bai, the recipe carries his mother's memory — something that can't be optimized or updated. Zhou's point about availability may be factually correct, but it crosses from cooking into something personal. Bai's silence protects what matters to him.

title: The Team-Building Activity
text_zh: 秦经理组织了一次团建活动。主题是“提高团队合作能力”。活动内容是大家一起做一顿饭。老周负责买菜。他买了三倍的量。罗老师负责分工。她写了一个四页的计划表，包括每个人的任务和时间安排。老白负责调味。他说调味要靠感觉，不能按照计划来。罗老师说没有计划怎么行。老白说有计划就不是做饭了，是考试。最后菜做好了，味道还行。秦经理说：“大家合作得很好。”小梅说：“我什么都没做。”秦经理说：“你的存在也是一种参与。”
text_pinyin: Qín jīnglǐ zǔzhī le yí cì tuánjiàn huódòng. Zhǔtí shì 'tígāo tuánduì hézuò nénglì'. Huódòng nèiróng shì dàjiā yìqǐ zuò yí dùn fàn. Lǎo Zhōu fùzé mǎi cài. Tā mǎi le sān bèi de liàng. Luó lǎoshī fùzé fēngōng. Tā xiě le yí gè sì yè de jìhuà biǎo, bāokuò měi gè rén de rènwu hé shíjiān ānpái. Lǎo Bái fùzé tiáowèi. Tā shuō tiáowèi yào kào gǎnjué, bù néng ànzhào jìhuà lái. Luó lǎoshī shuō méi yǒu jìhuà zěnme xíng. Lǎo Bái shuō yǒu jìhuà jiù bú shì zuòfàn le, shì kǎoshì. Zuìhòu cài zuò hǎo le, wèidào hái xíng. Qín jīnglǐ shuō: 'Dàjiā hézuò de hěn hǎo.' Xiǎo Méi shuō: 'Wǒ shénme dōu méi zuò.' Qín jīnglǐ shuō: 'Nǐ de cúnzài yě shì yì zhǒng cānyù.'
text_en: Manager Qin organized a team-building activity. The theme was 'improving teamwork.' The activity was cooking a meal together. Old Zhou was in charge of shopping. He bought triple the amount needed. Teacher Luo was in charge of delegation. She wrote a four-page plan including everyone's tasks and time schedule. Uncle Bai was in charge of seasoning. He said seasoning relies on feeling, you can't follow a plan. Luo said how can you not have a plan. Bai said if you follow a plan it's not cooking, it's an exam. In the end the food was done. It tasted fine. Qin said: 'Everyone cooperated very well.' Mei said: 'I didn't do anything.' Qin said: 'Your presence is also a form of participation.'

questions.q_zh: 秦经理最后说的话有什么意思？
questions.q_en: What is the meaning behind Qin's last remark?

options.text: 他用官方语言把没有参与说成了参与
options.pinyin: tā yòng guānfāng yǔyán bǎ méi yǒu cānyù shuō chéng le cānyù
options.text_en: he used official language to reframe non-participation as participation

options.pinyin: Xiǎo Méi zhēn de bāng le hěn duō
Qín jīnglǐ zài pīpíng Xiǎo Méi
tuánjiàn huódòng shībài le

questions.explanation: Qin's bureaucratic instinct is to make everything sound like a success. Mei's honest 'I didn't do anything' gets laundered through administrative language into 'your presence is participation.' Nobody was malicious — this is just how organizational language absorbs reality.

title: The Expert Opinion
text_zh: 小区要换电梯。秦经理请了一位专家来评估。专家看了半个小时，写了一份报告。报告的结论是：“建议进一步调研后再做决定。”方阿姨看了报告，说：“也就是说，我们花了三千块请人告诉我们，他也不知道。”秦经理说这不是“不知道”，是“专业的审慎”。方阿姨说：“审慎是好的。但是电梯已经停了两次了。”秦经理说按照流程还需要第二次评估。方阿姨说：“那第二位专家会不会也建议请第三位专家？”秦经理没有回答。不是因为他觉得方阿姨说得不对，是因为他知道答案很可能是“会的”。
text_pinyin: Xiǎoqū yào huàn diàntī. Qín jīnglǐ qǐng le yí wèi zhuānjiā lái pínggū. Zhuānjiā kàn le bàn gè xiǎoshí, xiě le yí fèn bàogào. Bàogào de jiélùn shì: 'Jiànyì jìnyíbù diào yán hòu zài zuò juédìng.' Fāng āyí kàn le bàogào, shuō: 'Yě jiù shì shuō, wǒmen huā le sānqiān kuài qǐng rén gàosu wǒmen, tā yě bù zhīdào.' Qín jīnglǐ shuō zhè bú shì 'bù zhīdào', shì 'zhuānyè de shěnshèn'. Fāng āyí shuō: 'Shěnshèn shì hǎo de. Dànshì diàntī yǐjīng tíng le liǎng cì le.' Qín jīnglǐ shuō ànzhào liúchéng hái xūyào dì èr cì pínggū. Fāng āyí shuō: 'Nà dì èr wèi zhuānjiā huì bu huì yě jiànyì qǐng dì sān wèi zhuānjiā?' Qín jīnglǐ méi yǒu huídá. Bú shì yīnwèi tā juéde Fāng āyí shuō de bú duì, shì yīnwèi tā zhīdào dá'àn hěn kěnéng shì 'huì de'.
text_en: The community needed to replace the elevator. Manager Qin hired an expert to assess it. The expert looked for half an hour and wrote a report. The conclusion: 'Recommend further investigation before making a decision.' Fang read the report and said: 'So we paid three thousand yuan for someone to tell us he doesn't know either.' Qin said this wasn't 'not knowing' — it was 'professional prudence.' Fang said: 'Prudence is fine. But the elevator has already stopped twice.' Qin said procedure requires a second evaluation. Fang said: 'Will the second expert also recommend hiring a third expert?' Qin didn't answer. Not because he thought Fang was wrong, but because he knew the answer was probably 'yes.'

questions.q_zh: 秦经理为什么没有回答方阿姨的问题？
questions.q_en: Why didn't Qin answer Fang's question?

options.text: 因为他知道她说的可能是对的
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā zhīdào tā shuō de kěnéng shì duì de
options.text_en: because he knew she was probably right

options.text: 因为他听不懂方阿姨的话
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā tīng bù dǒng Fāng āyí de huà

options.text: 因为他不关心电梯的问题
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā bù guānxīn diàntī de wèntí
options.text_en: because he doesn't care about the elevator

options.text: 因为他已经请了第三位专家
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā yǐjīng qǐng le dì sān wèi zhuānjiā
options.text_en: because he already hired a third expert

questions.explanation: Qin's silence is the most honest thing in the passage. He lives inside the system — he knows its loops. Fang named the loop out loud: experts recommending more experts, procedure delaying action. Qin's silence acknowledges the truth without breaking his procedural role.

title: The Restaurant Review
text_zh: 方阿姨和小梅去了一家新餐厅。装修很漂亮，菜单上每道菜都有一段故事。方阿姨看了菜单说：“一碗面条的故事比面条本身还长。”小梅翻到最后一页，说：“最贵的菜叫'回忆'。”方阿姨说：“是谁的回忆？”面上来了。碗很大，面很少。小梅用筷子拨了一下，说：“留白。”方阿姨点了点头。她们吃完了，没有吃饱。路上经过一个路边摊，方阿姨买了两个肉夹馍。没有故事，没有留白。小梅说：“这个好吃。”方阿姨说：“东西好不好，跟故事没有关系。”
text_pinyin: Fāng āyí hé Xiǎo Méi qù le yì jiā xīn cāntīng. Zhuāngxiū hěn piàoliang, càidān shàng měi dào cài dōu yǒu yí duàn gùshi. Fāng āyí kàn le càidān shuō: 'Yì wǎn miàntiáo de gùshi bǐ miàntiáo běnshēn hái cháng.' Xiǎo Méi fān dào zuìhòu yí yè, shuō: 'Zuì guì de cài jiào huíyì.' Fāng āyí shuō: 'Shì shéi de huíyì?' Miàn shànglái le. Wǎn hěn dà, miàn hěn shǎo. Xiǎo Méi yòng kuàizi bō le yíxià, shuō: 'Liúbái.' Fāng āyí diǎn le diǎn tóu. Tāmen chī wán le, méi yǒu chī bǎo. Lù shàng jīngguò yí gè lùbiān tān, Fāng āyí mǎi le liǎng gè ròujiāmó. Méi yǒu gùshi, méi yǒu liúbái. Xiǎo Méi shuō: 'Zhè ge hǎo chī.' Fāng āyí shuō: 'Dōngxi hǎo bu hǎo, gēn gùshi méi yǒu guānxi.'
text_en: Auntie Fang and Xiao Mei went to a new restaurant. Beautiful decor, every dish on the menu came with a story. Fang looked at the menu and said: 'The story for a bowl of noodles is longer than the noodles themselves.' Mei flipped to the last page: 'The most expensive dish is called Memory.' Fang said: 'Whose memory?' The noodles arrived. Big bowl, not much noodle. Mei poked at it with her chopsticks and said: 'Negative space.' Fang nodded. They finished eating. They weren't full. On the way home they passed a street stall and Fang bought two roujiamo. No story, no negative space. Mei said: 'This is good.' Fang said: 'Whether something is good has nothing to do with the story.'

questions.q_zh: 小梅说“留白”是什么意思？
questions.q_en: What did Mei mean by 'negative space'?

options.text: 碗里面条太少，像是故意的艺术效果
options.pinyin: wǎn lǐ miàntiáo tài shǎo, xiàng shì gùyì de yìshù xiàoguǒ
options.text_en: there were too few noodles, as if it were an intentional artistic effect

options.pinyin: wǎn shì báisè de
miàntiáo de wèidào hěn dàn
options.text_en: the noodles had a bland flavor

options.pinyin: cāntīng de qiángbì hěn kōng

questions.explanation: Mei's one-word critique is devastating: she borrows art vocabulary ('negative space') to describe a bowl that's mostly empty. It's deadpan precision — she's not complaining, just accurately naming what she sees. The contrast with the roujiamo makes the point: substance doesn't need a narrative.

title: The Community Garden
text_zh: 秦经理批准了社区花园项目。罗老师提出应该种花，因为花让人心情好，况且小区需要美化环境。老白说应该种菜，毕竟种菜有实际用处，花好看但不能吃。罗老师说：“不是所有东西都要有用。美本身就有价值。”老白说：“美不能当饭吃。何况种花还要花钱买肥料，种菜至少能省点菜钱。”罗老师说：“那公园也不能吃，图书馆也不能吃，要不要都拆了种地？”老白说：“你这是偷换概念。”罗老师说：“你这才是偷换概念。”秦经理说他会写一份报告，分析花和菜的可行性。三个月后报告还没写完。花园的地上长满了草。不是花也不是菜。是草。
text_pinyin: Qín jīnglǐ pīzhǔn le shèqū huāyuán xiàngmù. Luó lǎoshī tíchū yīnggāi zhòng huā, yīnwèi huā ràng rén xīnqíng hǎo, kuàngjǐ xiǎoqū xūyào měihuà huánjìng. Lǎo Bái shuō yīnggāi zhòng cài, bìjìng zhòng cài yǒu shíjì yòngchù, huā hǎokàn dàn bù néng chī. Luó lǎoshī shuō: 'Bú shì suǒyǒu dōngxi dōu yào yǒu yòng. Měi běnshēn jiù yǒu jiàzhí.' Lǎo Bái shuō: 'Měi bù néng dāng fàn chī. Hékuàng zhòng huā hái yào huā qián mǎi féiliào, zhòng cài zhìshǎo néng shěng diǎn cài qián.' Luó lǎoshī shuō: 'Nà gōngyuán yě bù néng chī, túshūguǎn yě bù néng chī, yào bu yào dōu chāi le zhòng dì?' Lǎo Bái shuō: 'Nǐ zhè shì tōu huàn gàiniàn.' Luó lǎoshī shuō: 'Nǐ zhè cái shì tōu huàn gàiniàn.' Qín jīnglǐ shuō tā huì xiě yí fèn bàogào, fēnxī huā hé cài de kěxíng xìng. Sān gè yuè hòu bàogào hái méi xiě wán. Huāyuán de dì shàng zhǎng mǎn le cǎo. Bú shì huā yě bú shì cài. Shì cǎo.
text_en: Manager Qin approved the community garden project. Teacher Luo proposed planting flowers — flowers improve mood, and besides, the complex needs beautification. Uncle Bai said they should plant vegetables — after all, vegetables are practical. Flowers look nice but you can't eat them. Luo said: 'Not everything needs to be useful. Beauty has value in itself.' Bai said: 'Beauty can't feed you. What's more, flowers need fertilizer too. Vegetables at least save grocery money.' Luo said: 'Parks can't feed you either. Neither can libraries. Should we tear those down and plant crops?' Bai said: 'You're changing the subject.' Luo said: 'You're the one changing the subject.' Qin said he would write a report analyzing the feasibility of flowers versus vegetables. Three months later the report still wasn't finished. The garden plot was covered in weeds. Not flowers, not vegetables. Weeds.

questions.q_zh: 最后一句“不是花也不是菜，是草”说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the last line 'Not flowers, not vegetables. Weeds.' illustrate?

options.text: 争论太久不做决定，结果比两个选择都差
options.pinyin: zhēnglùn tài jiǔ bù zuò juédìng, jiéguǒ bǐ liǎng gè xuǎnzé dōu chà
options.text_en: arguing too long without deciding leads to an outcome worse than either option

options.pinyin: cǎo bǐ huā hé cài dōu hǎo
options.text_en: weeds are better than flowers or vegetables

options.pinyin: Qín jīnglǐ gùyì bù xiě bàogào
options.text_en: Qin deliberately didn't write the report

options.pinyin: huāyuán bù yīnggāi jiàn
options.text_en: the garden shouldn't have been built

questions.explanation: Both Luo and Bai had defensible positions — aesthetic value versus practical value. But the debate consumed all the energy that could have gone into action. Qin's procedural instinct (write a report) added delay. The weeds are the real verdict: indecision is its own decision.

title: The Satisfaction Survey
text_zh: 秦经理发了一份社区满意度调查。十二道题，每道题五个选项，从“非常满意”到“非常不满意”。老周全选了“非常满意”，用了三分钟。罗老师写了六页补充意见，比问卷本身还长。老白说这种调查没有意义，然后花了四十分钟填，每道题都选了“一般”。小梅没有填。方阿姨填了，但在最后一道题“您对本次调查是否满意”旁边写了一个问号。秦经理收回问卷以后统计了结果：平均满意度百分之七十八。他把这个数字写进了报告。报告的标题是“社区居民满意度稳步提升”。去年是百分之七十六。小梅看了报告说：“提升了两个百分点。”方阿姨说：“嗯。样本量是多少？”秦经理说：“四份。”
text_pinyin: Qín jīnglǐ fā le yí fèn shèqū mǎnyì dù diàochá. Shí'èr dào tí, měi dào tí wǔ gè xuǎnxiàng, cóng 'fēicháng mǎnyì' dào 'fēicháng bù mǎnyì'. Lǎo Zhōu quán xuǎn le 'fēicháng mǎnyì', yòng le sān fēnzhōng. Luó lǎoshī xiě le liù yè bǔchōng yìjiàn, bǐ wènjuàn běnshēn hái cháng. Lǎo Bái shuō zhè zhǒng diàochá méi yǒu yìyì, ránhòu huā le sìshí fēnzhōng tián, měi dào tí dōu xuǎn le 'yìbān'. Xiǎo Méi méi yǒu tián. Fāng āyí tián le, dàn zài zuìhòu yí dào tí 'nín duì běn cì diàochá shìfǒu mǎnyì' pángbiān xiě le yí gè wènhào. Qín jīnglǐ shōu huí wènjuàn yǐhòu tǒngjì le jiéguǒ: píngjūn mǎnyì dù bǎi fēn zhī qīshíbā. Tā bǎ zhè ge shùzì xiě jìn le bàogào. Bàogào de biāotí shì 'Shèqū jūmín mǎnyì dù wěn bù tíshēng'. Qùnián shì bǎi fēn zhī qīshíliù. Xiǎo Méi kàn le bàogào shuō: 'Tíshēng le liǎng gè bǎi fēn diǎn.' Fāng āyí shuō: 'Èn. Yàngběn liàng shì duōshǎo?' Qín jīnglǐ shuō: 'Sì fèn.'
text_en: Manager Qin distributed a community satisfaction survey. Twelve questions, five options each, from 'very satisfied' to 'very dissatisfied.' Old Zhou checked 'very satisfied' for everything in three minutes. Teacher Luo wrote six pages of supplementary comments, longer than the survey itself. Uncle Bai said surveys like this are meaningless, then spent forty minutes filling it out, selecting 'average' for every question. Xiao Mei didn't fill it out. Auntie Fang filled it out but wrote a question mark next to the last question: 'Are you satisfied with this survey?' Qin collected the responses and calculated the results: average satisfaction rate 78%. He put this number in his report. The report title: 'Community Resident Satisfaction Shows Steady Improvement.' Last year was 76%. Mei read the report and said: 'A two-point increase.' Fang said: 'Mm. What was the sample size?' Qin said: 'Four.'

questions.q_zh: 这个故事讽刺的是什么？

options.text: 用很少的数据写出好看的报告
options.pinyin: yòng hěn shǎo de shùjù xiě chū hǎokàn de bàogào
options.text_en: producing impressive reports from very little data

options.pinyin: shèqū jūmín bù pèihé diàochá
options.text_en: community residents don't cooperate with surveys

options.pinyin: Qín jīnglǐ bú huì zuò shùxué
mǎnyì dù tài dī le

questions.explanation: Every character's response to the survey is perfectly in-character, and each one undermines its validity in a different way. Yet the system still produces a clean number (78%) and a confident headline ('steady improvement'). The comedy is in the gap between four messy data points and the polished report. Fang's final question — the sample size — is the nail.

title: The Renovation Consultation
text_zh: 方阿姨的邻居装修房子，请了三个设计师来出方案。第一个设计师说要打通客厅和厨房，“让空间呼吸”。第二个设计师说要保留隔断，“给生活留边界”。第三个设计师说无非就是墙拆不拆的问题，不用讲哲学。方阿姨全程在旁边喝茶。邻居问她觉得怎么样。方阿姨说：“你家的墙本来就不是承重墙。拆不拆跟呼吸没有关系，跟你做饭的油烟有关系。你做饭油大，不妨留着隔断。”邻居愣住了。三个设计师也没说话。方阿姨放下茶杯说：“我说完了。”后来邻居没请设计师。他问了楼下的装修工。装修工说了一句话：“拆了以后你老婆会嫌油烟大。”跟方阿姨说的一样。只是没有茶。
text_pinyin: Fāng āyí de línjū zhuāngxiū fángzi, qǐng le sān gè shèjìshī lái chū fāng'àn. Dì yī gè shèjìshī shuō yào dǎ tōng kètīng hé chúfáng, 'ràng kōngjiān hūxī'. Dì èr gè shèjìshī shuō yào bǎoliú géduan, 'gěi shēnghuó liú biānjiè'. Dì sān gè shèjìshī shuō wúfēi jiù shì qiáng chāi bù chāi de wèntí, bú yòng jiǎng zhéxué. Fāng āyí quán chéng zài pángbiān hē chá. Línjū wèn tā juéde zěnmeyàng. Fāng āyí shuō: 'Nǐ jiā de qiáng běnlái jiù bú shì chéngzhòng qiáng. Chāi bù chāi gēn hūxī méi yǒu guānxi, gēn nǐ zuòfàn de yóuyān yǒu guānxi. Nǐ zuòfàn yóu dà, bùfáng liú zhe géduan.' Línjū lèng zhù le. Sān gè shèjìshī yě méi shuōhuà. Fāng āyí fàng xia chá bēi shuō: 'Wǒ shuō wán le.' Hòulái línjū méi qǐng shèjìshī. Tā wèn le lóuxià de zhuāngxiū gōng. Zhuāngxiū gōng shuō le yí jù huà: 'Chāi le yǐhòu nǐ lǎopó huì xián yóuyān dà.' Gēn Fāng āyí shuō de yíyàng. Zhǐ shì méi yǒu chá.
text_en: Fang's neighbor was renovating and invited three designers to pitch. The first said to open up the living room and kitchen — 'let the space breathe.' The second said to keep the partition — 'give life its boundaries.' The third said it was simply a question of whether to knock down the wall or not, no philosophy needed. Fang sat to the side drinking tea the entire time. The neighbor asked what she thought. Fang said: 'Your wall isn't load-bearing to begin with. Whether to remove it has nothing to do with breathing — it has to do with cooking fumes. You cook with a lot of oil. You might want to keep the partition.' The neighbor was stunned. The three designers said nothing. Fang put down her teacup: 'I'm done.' Later the neighbor didn't hire a designer. He asked the renovation worker downstairs. The worker said one sentence: 'If you knock it down, your wife will complain about the cooking fumes.' Same as what Fang said. Just without tea.

options.text: 实际经验比理论包装更有用
options.pinyin: shíjì jīngyàn bǐ lǐlùn bāozhuāng gèng yǒu yòng
options.text_en: practical experience is more useful than theoretical packaging

options.pinyin: shèjìshī dōu bù zhuānyè
Fāng āyí yīnggāi dāng shèjìshī
géduan yídìng bù néng chāi

questions.explanation: Three designers each framed the same question (wall or no wall) through abstract concepts — breathing, boundaries, philosophy. Fang and the renovation worker cut through to the actual variable: cooking oil smoke. The sharpness is in seeing that the real question was never about space or philosophy.

title: The Compliment
text_zh: 老周做了一锅汤，端到社区活动室让大家尝。大家都喝了。老周问每个人觉得怎么样。罗老师说：“很有层次感。”老白说：“这个汤有深度。”秦经理说：“整体来看，表现稳定。”小梅说：“嗯。”方阿姨没有说话。老周问方阿姨：“你觉得呢？”方阿姨说：“盐放多了。”老周愣了一下。方阿姨继续说：“但是鸡汤的底子是好的。索性下次少放一半盐，味道会好很多。”老周高兴了。不是因为方阿姨夸了他，而是因为终于有人告诉他具体哪里要改。后来老周改了。汤确实好了。罗老师喝了以后说：“嗯，层次感更丰富了。”方阿姨看了小梅一眼。小梅没有看方阿姨。但她们都知道层次感跟盐没有关系。
text_pinyin: Lǎo Zhōu zuò le yì guō tāng, duān dào shèqū huódòng shì ràng dàjiā cháng. Dàjiā dōu hē le. Lǎo Zhōu wèn měi gè rén juéde zěnmeyàng. Luó lǎoshī shuō: 'Hěn yǒu céngcì gǎn.' Lǎo Bái shuō: 'Zhè ge tāng yǒu shēndù.' Qín jīnglǐ shuō: 'Zhěngtǐ lái kàn, biǎoxiàn wěndìng.' Xiǎo Méi shuō: 'Èn.' Fāng āyí méi yǒu shuōhuà. Lǎo Zhōu wèn Fāng āyí: 'Nǐ juéde ne?' Fāng āyí shuō: 'Yán fàng duō le.' Lǎo Zhōu lèng le yíxià. Fāng āyí jìxù shuō: 'Dànshì jītāng de dǐzi shì hǎo de. Suǒxìng xià cì shǎo fàng yí bàn yán, wèidào huì hǎo hěn duō.' Lǎo Zhōu gāoxìng le. Bú shì yīnwèi Fāng āyí kuā le tā, ér shì yīnwèi zhōngyú yǒu rén gàosu tā jùtǐ nǎlǐ yào gǎi. Hòulái Lǎo Zhōu gǎi le. Tāng quèshí hǎo le. Luó lǎoshī hē le yǐhòu shuō: 'Èn, céngcì gǎn gèng fēngfù le.' Fāng āyí kàn le Xiǎo Méi yì yǎn. Xiǎo Méi méi yǒu kàn Fāng āyí. Dàn tāmen dōu zhīdào céngcì gǎn gēn yán méi yǒu guānxi.
text_en: Old Zhou made a pot of soup and brought it to the community center for everyone to try. Everyone tasted it. Zhou asked each person what they thought. Teacher Luo said: 'Very layered.' Uncle Bai said: 'This soup has depth.' Manager Qin said: 'Overall, a stable performance.' Xiao Mei said: 'Mm.' Auntie Fang didn't speak. Zhou asked Fang: 'What do you think?' Fang said: 'Too much salt.' Zhou froze for a moment. Fang continued: 'But the chicken stock base is good. You might as well use half the salt next time — the flavor will improve a lot.' Zhou was happy. Not because Fang praised him, but because someone finally told him specifically what to fix. Later Zhou adjusted. The soup was genuinely better. Teacher Luo tasted it and said: 'Mm, the layers are even richer now.' Fang glanced at Mei. Mei didn't look at Fang. But they both knew 'layers' had nothing to do with salt.

questions.q_zh: 为什么老周听了方阿姨的话反而高兴了？
questions.q_en: Why was Zhou actually happy after hearing Fang's critique?

options.text: 因为她是唯一给了具体可以改进的建议的人
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā shì wéiyī gěi le jùtǐ kěyǐ gǎijìn de jiànyì de rén
options.text_en: because she was the only one who gave specific, actionable feedback

options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā xǐhuān bèi pīpíng
yīnwèi Fāng āyí hěn shǎo shuōhuà
yīnwèi tāng quèshí tài xián le
options.text_en: because the soup was indeed too salty

questions.explanation: Everyone else's response sounded positive but was actually meaningless — 'layered,' 'depth,' 'stable performance' are ways of saying nothing specific. Fang's 'too much salt' is the only feedback that can be acted on. The proof: Zhou used it, improved, and Luo still said 'layered' — the same word, applied to a different soup, proving it was never about the actual taste.

title: The Night Shift
text_zh: 便利店的夜班只有一个人。小林做了三年夜班。他跟秦经理说想换到白班。秦经理说目前没有白班的空位，不过可以提交调岗申请。小林说他已经提了两次了。秦经理翻了翻记录，说确实有两次，但两次都因为“排班周期内无法调整”被驳回了。小林说：“排班周期永远都不会刚好合适。”秦经理沉默了一下，说：“制度是这样设计的。”小林说：“制度设计的时候没有问过做夜班的人。”秦经理没有反驳。他知道小林说的是事实。但他也知道，如果每个人都绕过制度，那制度就不存在了。问题不在于谁有道理。问题在于，有道理的人依然要在凌晨三点一个人站在收银台后面。
text_pinyin: Biànlì diàn de yèbān zhǐ yǒu yí gè rén. Xiǎo Lín zuò le sān nián yèbān. Tā gēn Qín jīnglǐ shuō xiǎng huàn dào bái bān. Qín jīnglǐ shuō mùqián méi yǒu bái bān de kòng wèi, búguò kěyǐ tíjiāo diào gǎng shēnqǐng. Xiǎo Lín shuō tā yǐjīng tí le liǎng cì le. Qín jīnglǐ fān le fān jìlù, shuō quèshí yǒu liǎng cì, dàn liǎng cì dōu yīnwèi 'páibān zhōuqī nèi wúfǎ tiáozhěng' bèi bóhuí le. Xiǎo Lín shuō: 'Páibān zhōuqī yǒngyuǎn dōu bú huì gānghǎo héshì.' Qín jīnglǐ chénmò le yíxià, shuō: 'Zhìdù shì zhèyàng shèjì de.' Xiǎo Lín shuō: 'Zhìdù shèjì de shíhou méi yǒu wèn guò zuò yèbān de rén.' Qín jīnglǐ méi yǒu fǎnbó. Tā zhīdào Xiǎo Lín shuō de shì shìshí. Dàn tā yě zhīdào, rúguǒ měi gè rén dōu rào guò zhìdù, nà zhìdù jiù bù cúnzài le. Wèntí bú zàiyú shéi yǒu dàolǐ. Wèntí zàiyú, yǒu dàolǐ de rén yīrán yào zài língchén sān diǎn yí gè rén zhàn zài shōuyín tái hòumiàn.
text_en: The convenience store night shift has only one person. Xiao Lin has worked nights for three years. He told Manager Qin he wanted to switch to days. Qin said there were no openings on the day shift, but he could submit a transfer request. Lin said he'd already submitted two. Qin checked the records — confirmed, two requests, both rejected because 'adjustments cannot be made within the current scheduling cycle.' Lin said: 'The scheduling cycle will never happen to be convenient.' Qin was silent for a moment, then said: 'The system was designed this way.' Lin said: 'When they designed the system, they didn't ask the people working nights.' Qin didn't argue back. He knew Lin was stating fact. But he also knew that if everyone bypassed the system, the system wouldn't exist. The question isn't who has a point. The question is that the person who has a point still has to stand behind the register alone at 3 a.m.

questions.q_zh: 最后一句话表达了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the last sentence express?

options.text: 有道理不代表能改变现状
options.pinyin: yǒu dàolǐ bù dàibiǎo néng gǎibiàn xiànzhuàng
options.text_en: being right doesn't mean you can change the situation

options.pinyin: Xiǎo Lín yīnggāi cízhí
Qín jīnglǐ bù gōngpíng
yèbān bù xīnkǔ

questions.explanation: Neither person is wrong. Lin's critique of the system is accurate — it wasn't designed with input from the people it affects most. Qin's defense is also real — systems require consistency. The sting is in the final image: being right doesn't keep you warm at 3 a.m. The passage doesn't resolve; it just presents the weight.

title: The Dialect
text_zh: 罗老师在社区文化活动上提议开一个方言课，教小区里的孩子们本地方言。她说：“方言是文化的根。如果孩子们不会说方言，等老人不在了，这些话就永远消失了。”老白举手赞成。他说他小时候全家说方言，现在连他自己都说不利索了。方阿姨没有举手。她说：“我同意方言重要。但是我想问一个问题：这些孩子以后要考试、要工作、要跟全国各地的人打交道。他们的时间是有限的。学方言的时间从哪里来？从英语课里来？从数学课里来？”罗老师说：“你不能用功利的眼光看文化。”方阿姨说：“我不是功利。我是在替那些孩子的家长想。”没有人能反驳这句话。因为在座的人都没有正在上学的孩子。他们在替别人的孩子做选择。这件事本身就是问题的一部分。
text_pinyin: Luó lǎoshī zài shèqū wénhuà huódòng shàng tíyì kāi yí gè fāngyán kè, jiāo xiǎoqū lǐ de háizimen běndì fāngyán. Tā shuō: 'Fāngyán shì wénhuà de gēn. Rúguǒ háizimen bú huì shuō fāngyán, děng lǎorén bú zài le, zhèxiē huà jiù yǒngyuǎn xiāoshī le.' Lǎo Bái jǔshǒu zànchéng. Tā shuō tā xiǎo shíhou quán jiā shuō fāngyán, xiànzài lián tā zìjǐ dōu shuō bù lìsuo le. Fāng āyí méi yǒu jǔshǒu. Tā shuō: 'Wǒ tóngyì fāngyán zhòngyào. Dànshì wǒ xiǎng wèn yí gè wèntí: zhèxiē háizi yǐhòu yào kǎoshì, yào gōngzuò, yào gēn quánguó gèdì de rén dǎjiāodào. Tāmen de shíjiān shì yǒuxiàn de. Xué fāngyán de shíjiān cóng nǎlǐ lái? Cóng yīngyǔ kè lǐ lái? Cóng shùxué kè lǐ lái?' Luó lǎoshī shuō: 'Nǐ bù néng yòng gōnglì de yǎnguāng kàn wénhuà.' Fāng āyí shuō: 'Wǒ bú shì gōnglì. Wǒ shì zài tì nàxiē háizi de jiāzhǎng xiǎng.' Méi yǒu rén néng fǎnbó zhè jù huà. Yīnwèi zàizuò de rén dōu méi yǒu zhèngzài shàngxué de háizi. Tāmen zài tì biéren de háizi zuò xuǎnzé. Zhè jiàn shì běnshēn jiù shì wèntí de yí bùfèn.
text_en: At a community cultural event, Teacher Luo proposed starting a dialect class to teach local children the regional dialect. She said: 'Dialect is the root of culture. If children can't speak dialect, once the elderly are gone, those words will disappear forever.' Uncle Bai raised his hand in support. He said when he was young the whole family spoke dialect; now even he can't speak it fluently. Auntie Fang didn't raise her hand. She said: 'I agree dialect is important. But I want to ask a question: these children will have exams, careers, they'll need to interact with people from all over the country. Their time is finite. Where does dialect study time come from? From English class? From math class?' Luo said: 'You can't look at culture through a utilitarian lens.' Fang said: 'I'm not being utilitarian. I'm thinking on behalf of those children's parents.' Nobody could counter that statement. Because no one present had school-age children. They were making choices for other people's children. That fact itself was part of the problem.

questions.q_zh: 最后为什么说“这件事本身就是问题的一部分”？
questions.q_en: Why does the passage say 'that fact itself was part of the problem'?

options.text: 做决定的人不受这个决定的影响
options.pinyin: zuò juédìng de rén bù shòu zhè ge juédìng de yǐngxiǎng
options.text_en: the decision-makers aren't affected by the decision

options.pinyin: zàizuò de rén bù dǒng fāngyán
options.text_en: the people present don't understand dialect

options.text: 社区活动不应该讨论教育
options.pinyin: shèqū huódòng bù yīnggāi tǎolùn jiàoyù
háizimen bù xiǎng xué fāngyán
options.text_en: the children don't want to learn dialect

questions.explanation: Both positions are genuine — Luo's cultural preservation argument and Fang's practical time-constraint argument are both real. But the final observation cuts deeper than either: the people debating have no skin in the game. They're deciding what other people's children should spend time on, without bearing the consequences. The structural problem isn't which side is right — it's who gets to decide.
